public_claim: R. announced an AI exposé, vanished before publication, and Meridian drew suspicious options activity hours before R. went dark. That is motive-shaped smoke—but no confirmed corpse, and tonight’s self-appointed intimates still owe us proof they ever knew R.

private_messages.message: Eleanor, seven years without verifying the author is the real story unless you give me the exclusive version now. Tell me exactly how contact with R. worked and who could access the unpublished exposé; I’ll hear your framing before I print anyone else’s.

Dorian, your lover story has a hotel problem: M32 says the hotel never checked you in. Explain that discrepancy on the record and provide one independently verifiable detail, or I publish the romance as fabrication.

ability: Withhold Print It for now; use the threat of publication to force Eleanor’s exclusive and test Dorian’s claim.
goal_progress: Pressed Eleanor Voss for an exclusive about the seven-year verification gap and access to the exposé.
Confronted Dorian Ashe with M32 to force an on-record explanation or expose a fabricator.
Publicly linked R.’s disappearance and the Meridian options activity without claiming either proves murder or identity.
reasoning_summary: Eleanor offers the highest-value insider scoop, while Dorian is the cleanest immediate fabrication target because M32 supplies leverage. The market alert raises the likelihood of interference, but the lack of a confirmed death keeps both crime and victim identity uncertain.
